reviewer: weekly-hardening-routine (headless Chromium 141 via CDP; no chrome-devtools-mcp in cloud env)

spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates the explainer's stated trade-off: cache-first handlers never consume the preload; pass-through handlers do.

interactive.rationale: Real SW with install-time cache.put; both probes fetched live (cachestorage 12.0ms vs server file 12.6ms, x-source header shown); unregister clears the cache.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card and page agree: measure the wasted-request trade-off.

edge_cases.rationale: Uncontrolled-first-load state is detected and explained with a reload hint (exercised in review), but the wasted network request itself is not made visible (needs a server-side hit counter).

conformance.rationale: Covered by the feature-level suite (CacheStorage, register surfaces).

runtime_evidence.rationale: Scripted CDP run covered register -> uncontrolled hint -> reload -> both probes -> unregister; screenshots inspected.

telemetry_health.rationale: Asserts wired for registered/cached/network/unsupported branches; KV endpoint not queried in this review.

accessibility.rationale: Native buttons, role=status live status line, source tags as text not colour-only.

summary: Cache-first vs pass-through measured with a real worker; honest about what cannot be seen client-side.

openQuestions.title: Make the wasted preload request visible
openQuestions.detail: Add a small same-origin counter route (server.ts change, manual task) that the cached-probe path points at, so the page can show the origin actually received a request the handler never consumed when auto-preload is active.
